name: Forcadas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Cedeira - vuelta por Embalse de Forcadas
description: Cycle 26.0 miles (41.9 km) around Embalse de Forcadas from Cedeira, gaining 2561 feet (780 metres) on this moderate road bike route.
region_intro: Road cycling around Cedeira offers diverse terrain characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs, the Serra da Capelada mountain range, and the Cedeira estuary. The region features some of Europe's highest cliffs, including Garita de Herbeira, which stands over 614 meters above sea level. Cyclists can navigate kilometers of asphalt roads, combining challenging climbs into the hills with scenic coastal routes. This varied landscape provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Cedeira The most popular road cycling route is Forcadas Reservoir Loop, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Embalse de Forcadas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Road along cliffs – Climb to La Garita loop from Vilarrube, a difficult 40.9 miles (65.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and coastal vistas along the high cliffs of the region. Local road cyclists also love the Viewpoint over the Cedeira estuary – View of Praia de Vilarrube loop from Cedeira, a 24.6 miles (39.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Cedeira estuary, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Cedeira is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, the Serra da Capelada mountains, and the Cedeira estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Cedeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cedeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cedeira?
A: Cedeira offers a diverse selection of over 40 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 19 moderate and 21 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cedeira?
A: The terrain around Cedeira is remarkably varied, characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs, the majestic Serra da Capelada mountain range, and the serene Cedeira estuary. You'll find routes combining challenging climbs into the hills with scenic coastal roads, offering a unique blend of landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Cedeira?
A: Yes, Cedeira is well-suited for experienced road cyclists, offering 21 difficult routes. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road along cliffs – Climb to La Garita loop from Vilarrube&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 65.8 km with over 1440 meters of elevation gain, providing significant challenges and stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any moderate or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Cedeira?
A: Yes, Cedeira offers 19 moderate road cycling routes that are suitable for those looking for less intense rides or beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969758816/forcadas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forcadas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, spanning 41.9 km with 780 meters of elevation gain, offering scenic views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints accessible by road bike in Cedeira?
A: Cedeira's routes are rich with scenic viewpoints. You can cycle to breathtaking spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/425141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garita Herbeira (611m)&lt;/a&gt;, one of Europe's highest cliffs, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/425142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teixidelo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24830779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Cedeira estuary – View of Praia de Vilarrube loop from Cedeira&lt;/a&gt;, offer unforgettable views of the estuary and its cove.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes in Cedeira?
A: Yes, many of Cedeira's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969758816/forcadas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forcadas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the various routes offering views over the Cedeira estuary, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Praia de Vilarrube – Viewpoint over the Cedeira estuary loop from Cedeira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Cedeira?
A: The region's mild climate makes road cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, especially on climbs, but coastal breezes provide relief. Winter cycling is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potential rain, particularly in exposed coastal areas.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about Cedeira?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Cedeira, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic coastal cliffs, the challenging climbs of the Serra da Capelada, and the quiet asphalt roads that lead to stunning, untouched landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see near the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore significant landmarks. The mythical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of San Andrés de Teixido&lt;/a&gt;, nestled in the Sierra de A Capelada, is a must-see pilgrimage site. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3479074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Ortegal Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; for spectacular coastal views or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; for a natural wonder.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, Cedeira and the smaller towns along the coast offer various establishments where you can stop for refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these villages will ensure opportunities for breaks and local culinary experiences.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the road cycling routes in Cedeira?
A: Yes, Cedeira boasts abundant and pristine beaches such as Praia da Magdalena, Arealonga, and Soreiras. Many road cycling routes run along the coast or pass near these beaches, offering opportunities for a refreshing dip in the Atlantic after your ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Cedeira?
A: Cedeira, being a coastal town, generally offers parking options, especially near the town center or along the waterfront. For routes starting outside the main town, look for designated parking areas in smaller villages or near popular trailheads. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.
tips: Small fishing village on the Rio Cederia estuary on the north coast of the Rias Altas de Glaician
The construction of the Forcadas reservoir began in 1962 and lasted 4 years creating a hydroelectric dam on the Forcadas river

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for the Cedeira - tour around Embalse de Forcadas?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is rated as moderate. It covers approximately 41.9 kilometers (26 miles) with an elevation gain of about 780 meters (2,559 feet). While the surfaces are generally well-paved, the significant ascents mean it requires a good level of fitness to complete comfortably.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in Cedeira. You can typically find parking within the town of Cedeira, which serves as a convenient starting point for exploring the route around Embalse de Forcadas.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cedeira - tour around Embalse de Forcadas is designed as a circular route, bringing you back to your starting point in Cedeira after cycling around the reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved road surfaces throughout the tour. The route features notable elevation gains, so be prepared for ascents and descents. The landscape typically includes rolling hills, and potentially coastal views as you cycle through the Cedeira area.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this tour include the Cedeira Beach and Bridge, offering scenic coastal views, and the Forcadas Reservoir Bridge, which provides views over the Embalse de Forcadas itself.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Cedeira area in Galicia generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during the spring and autumn months. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring more rain, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended for the best experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route, especially within ZEC Ortigueira-Mera?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. While parts of the area fall within the ZEC Ortigueira-Mera (Special Area of Conservation), standard public access for activities like road cycling is usually permitted without special authorization. Always respect local regulations and natural environments.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often welcome in outdoor areas, this specific tour is designed for racebikes on paved roads. Bringing a dog along for this type of cycling tour might be challenging due to the speed and distance. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely keep up or is secured in a suitable trailer, and always keep it on a leash when off the bike.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it does briefly intersect or run alongside sections of other paths. You might encounter very short segments that are part of the Mtb A. Teixido, Senda das Ondas, or the Paseo Marítimo.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the Embalse de Forcadas section of the route?
faqs.answer: The main services, including cafes and restaurants, are primarily located in the town of Cedeira, where the tour begins and ends. Along the Embalse de Forcadas section itself, amenities are limited, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike tour, you should bring your road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump), and a charged phone. Given the elevation changes, consider bringing layers of clothing.
